meta.title: CryptoLab · Local cryptography and secure sharing
meta.description: Encode, hash, encrypt, inspect JWTs, and share public-key encrypted QR messages locally in your browser

header.subtitle: Local cryptography, secure sharing, and JWT utilities

footer.privacy: All computation stays in your browser; no data leaves the page.

share.title: Encrypt a message for one recipient
share.intro: The recipient keeps the private key and shares the public key. The sender creates a QR code or packet. A random AES key encrypts the message, then RSA wraps that AES key.
share.factPrivate: Private key never enters the QR code
share.clearHint: Clear keys, messages, packets, QR output, and decrypted content

keys.title: Recipient prepares keys
keys.detail: Generate a 2048-bit RSA-OAEP key pair. Share only the public key with the sender; keep or safely back up the private key on the recipient side.
keys.publicPlaceholder: Generate or paste a PEM public key
keys.fingerprintHint: Compare the full fingerprint with the recipient over another trusted channel; a fingerprint alone does not prove identity.
keys.privatePlaceholder: Generate or paste a PEM private key
keys.privateWarning: Anyone with this private key can decrypt packets sent to it. CryptoLab does not save keys; store it offline because a lost key cannot be recovered.

encrypt.title: Sender creates a QR code
encrypt.detail: Paste the recipient public key and enter a message. Every run creates a new AES-256-GCM key and random IV, so identical messages produce different packets.
encrypt.placeholder: Enter a short message; QR works best for credentials, addresses, and brief instructions
encrypt.qrAlt: QR code containing a CL1 encrypted packet
encrypt.qrHint: The QR contains only ciphertext, a random IV, and the AES key wrapped by the public key.

decrypt.title: Recipient imports and decrypts
decrypt.detail: Upload the received QR image or .cryptolab packet, or paste the packet. Only the matching private key can complete authenticated decryption.
decrypt.importHint: PNG / JPEG / WebP / .cryptolab / TXT, up to 8 MB
decrypt.packetPlaceholder: Paste a packet beginning with CL1.
decrypt.privateReminder: The private key is used only in this page's memory

status.keysReady: Keys were generated locally. Back up the private key safely before sharing the public key.
status.packetReady: The QR code and packet were generated locally.
status.imported: The file was read and its CL1 packet structure was validated.
status.decrypted: Authenticated decryption succeeded; no packet tampering was detected.

errors.message-too-large: The message exceeds the 1,024-byte safe QR budget.
errors.invalid-packet: This is not a valid CL1 packet, or a field violates its safety limits.
errors.packet-too-large: The packet exceeds the safe QR capacity.
errors.encryption-failed: This public key could not encrypt the message; check that it is an RSA-OAEP PEM public key.
errors.decryption-failed: Decryption failed: the private key does not match, or the packet is damaged or tampered with.
errors.file-too-large: The file exceeds the 8 MB import limit.
errors.unsupported-file: Only PNG, JPEG, WebP, .cryptolab, and TXT are supported.
errors.image-too-large: The image exceeds the local pixel safety budget.
errors.image-unreadable: The browser could not read this image.
errors.qr-not-found: No usable QR code was found in the image.
errors.key-generation: This browser could not generate an RSA key pair.
errors.import-failed: The file could not be read.

about.title: About CryptoLab
about.intro: CryptoLab is a local workbench for development, learning, and short-message exchange. It keeps the boundaries between encoding, hashing, and encryption explicit.

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.body: Encoding and radix tools, digests and HMAC, symmetric encryption, RSA, JWT inspection, and CL1 secure sharing live in one workbench. The knowledge base explains the capability and limits of each family.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.body: Messages, keys, tokens, QR codes, and files exist only in current browser memory. There are no accounts, backend, telemetry, ads, cookies, remote fonts, or business network requests. Refreshing clears the content.

security.title: Security model
security.body: Secure share uses RSA-OAEP/SHA-256 to wrap a random AES-256-GCM key and binds a fixed context as additional authenticated data. CL1 packets enforce strict version, length, and format checks; private keys never enter QR codes or URLs.

limits.title: Limits
limits.body: QR messages are limited to 1,024 UTF-8 bytes. The tool does not provide identity authentication, key custody, revocation, forward secrecy, or protection from a compromised device. AES-CBC, raw ChaCha20, MD5, and SHA-1 are compatibility and learning tools only.

about.disclaimer: Do not treat an experimental utility as a key-management system. High-value or long-lived keys belong in audited hardware, a password manager, or an organizational security process.

hash.inputPlaceholder: Type text to compute all hashes in real time

notice.aesGcm: Recommended for new data. Use a unique random IV for every key; the authentication tag detects a wrong key or tampering.
notice.aesCbc: Compatibility only. CBC does not authenticate integrity; do not use this output in a new protocol without a correct Encrypt-then-MAC design.
notice.chacha20: Learning and compatibility only. This is raw ChaCha20 without Poly1305 authentication; reusing a key and nonce breaks confidentiality.

rsa.purposeNotice: Changing purpose clears the current keys and results so the same key material is not reused for encryption and signing.
rsa.encryptionPlaceholder: Enter short plaintext to encrypt or Base64 ciphertext to decrypt
rsa.signingPlaceholder: Enter the exact original message to sign or verify
rsa.signaturePlaceholder: Signing writes here; paste a Base64 signature to verify
rsa.oaepBytes: {{current}} plaintext bytes; this key allows up to {{max}}
rsa.messageTooLong: The plaintext is {{current}} UTF-8 bytes, above this key's {{max}}-byte OAEP limit.
rsa.pemTooLarge: The PEM key exceeds the safe input limit.

jwt.secretHint: Local HS256 / HS512 verification only. The secret is hidden by default and is never saved.
jwt.inputTooLarge: The token exceeds the 64 KiB safe input limit.

verification.title: Signature status
verification.unverified: Decoded only; the signature has not been verified. Do not trust the payload on this basis.
verification.valid: The HS signature is valid; the application must still check issuer, audience, and authorization rules.
verification.invalid: The signature is invalid or the algorithm does not match the secret.
verification.unsupported: Decoded; this panel does not verify {{algorithm}} signatures.

time.title: Standard time claims
time.none: No exp, nbf, or iat claims are present.
time.future-issued: Issued-at time is in the future · {{date}}

tip.md5: 128-bit hash, broken. Legacy checksums only; never for security.
tip.sha1: 160-bit hash, broken. Use SHA-256 or SHA-3 for new projects.
tip.sha256: 256-bit hash, mainstream. Used in TLS, blockchain, etc.
tip.sha3: Keccak sponge, NIST 2015. Structurally distinct from SHA-2, length-extension resistant.
tip.hmac: Keyed hash that verifies both integrity and authenticity. Only key holders can produce valid HMAC.
tip.aesGcm: AES + GCM mode = AEAD. Built-in auth tag detects tampering. Used in TLS 1.3.
tip.aesCbc: AES + CBC mode, legacy block cipher. No built-in auth; pair with HMAC.
tip.chacha20: A stream cipher that can outperform AES without hardware acceleration. This panel implements raw ChaCha20 without Poly1305 authentication.
tip.rsaKeySize: 1024-bit deprecated; 2048-bit minimum; 4096-bit offers more margin but is slower. Use separate key pairs for RSA-OAEP encryption and RSA-PSS signing.
tip.jwt: JWT = Header.Payload.Signature. Payload is Base64Url-encoded, not encrypted — anyone can read it. Signature guarantees integrity, not confidentiality.
